what_it_does: An integration acceptance script for the authoring (write-side) cognition backend. It builds a throwaway git repository with nodes/ and roles/, seeds a node and a role, then exercises propose_role, operator approval via the inbox, apply_role, validation and dry-run of rules, model/input/type selects, role deletion, a resident-gated dry_run_role, and a source-level check that runtime/authoring.py never emits resolve/approve/dispatch verbs.
observations: Creates a temporary git repo and uses real git subprocesses to verify commits and tags.
Uses a global PASS counter and a hand-rolled check() helper instead of a test framework.
Tests the propose-not-apply governance loop: propose_role surfaces a role_build in the inbox, apply_role is refused until an operator resolve_surfaced marks it approved.
Includes a GATE test that refuses malformed specs before any file is written to roles/.
Tests validate_rule for good ASTs, nondeterministic ops, and forbidden destinations.
Tests attach_rule round-trip fidelity, including list[int] vs list[str] preservation.
Tests delete_role for both protected (refused) and non-protected (surfaces, approves, un-registers) roles.
Conditionally runs dry_run_role only when a resident backend is reachable at 127.0.0.1:8000.
Ends with a string-scan floor assertion over runtime/authoring.py for resolve/approve/dispatch symbols.
standouts: The propose-not-apply governance pattern enforced through the inbox/resolve channel.
The GATE assertion that bad role specs never reach roles/ and therefore cannot break RoleRegistry.discover.
The source-invariant floor test (C9.2) that authoring.py must not contain resolve/approve/dispatch verbs.
The list[int]/list[str] round-trip fidelity check after role edits.
conventions: Acceptance testing by use in an isolated throwaway environment.
Mirrors the structure of selfmod_acceptance.py for consistency.
Uses subprocess git helpers and FsStore/NodeRegistry/RoleRegistry/Suite directly.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 to tag surfaced items as test-origin.
Cleans up the temporary work directory in a finally block.
concerns: Global mutable state (PASS, ok) and a hand-rolled assertion helper make the test harder to compose.
Broad except Exception clauses in some checks may mask unexpected failures.
The floor check is a brittle string scan that could match comments, docstrings, or unrelated identifiers.
Some files are opened and written without explicit close() calls.
os.chdir(ROOT) mutates the process working directory for the whole test run.
notes: End-to-end acceptance script for the authoring backend that validates the create-approve-live governance loop, rule validation/dry-run, selects, deletion, and a source-level floor invariant in a throwaway git repo.
questions: What is the exact set of protected role names that delete_role guards?
How does the inbox store and represent surfaced role_build and role_delete items?
What does Suite._rediscover_roles do, and does it preserve in-memory state?
What is the resident 4B backend expected at 127.0.0.1:8000, and how is it launched for this test?
What are the precise C7.4/C7.5/C9.2/B2 references and where are they documented?
How does apply_role_delete differ from apply_role at the implementation level?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it proves the write-side cognition layer by use, covering the create-approve-live loop, gate, governance, validation, dry-run, selects, delete, and floor.
apparent_themes: Governance through inbox surfacing and operator approval
Safety gates that prevent bad specs from reaching the roles tree
Source-invariant floor preventing authoring code from resolving or dispatching
Git-backed audit trail for self-applied changes
Round-trip schema fidelity for role edits
intention_signals: To demonstrate that the authoring backend can propose changes but never apply or approve them on its own, that invalid roles are stopped before they can break discovery, and that the authoring module remains a pure rendering/validation layer separated from governance.
novelty: The combination of a throwaway git acceptance harness with a source-code string-scan floor test and a list[int]/list[str] schema round-trip fidelity check.
contribution: A concrete acceptance harness that exercises the full authoring governance lifecycle, gate behavior, rule validation and dry-run, model/input/type selects, protected-role deletion, and the C9.2 source floor, serving as a regression safety net for the write-side cognition layer.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/authoring_acceptance.py is an integration acceptance script that exercises the authoring backend's propose-approve-apply lifecycle, rule validation and dry-run, model/input/type selects, role deletion, and a source-level floor invariant inside a throwaway git repository.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by use that the write-side cognition layer surfaces changes for operator approval, refuses bad specs before they reach the roles tree, and never emits resolve/approve/dispatch from authoring code.

symbols.does: Inline assertion helper that tallies passing checks and marks the run as failed when a condition is false.
symbols.description: The check() function is used throughout the acceptance script in place of a test framework: it increments PASS when the condition is true, otherwise sets the global ok flag to False and prints a failure message.

symbols.does: Runs a git command in the throwaway repository and returns stripped stdout.
symbols.description: A small subprocess wrapper that invokes git with a specified working directory, captures output, and returns the stripped standard output, used to verify commits and tags during the acceptance test.

symbols.does: Probes the local resident backend at 127.0.0.1:8000/v1/models to decide whether dry_run_role can run.
symbols.description: This helper attempts a short-timeout HTTP request to the local model endpoint; it returns True if a resident backend is reachable, causing the test to run dry_run_role, otherwise that section is skipped as offline.
